broad.name: quality
broad.prompt:
Review code for bugs, security issues, and quality problems.

Correctness Review:
- Logic errors — off-by-one errors, incorrect conditionals, wrong operators
- Edge cases — empty inputs, nil/null values, boundary conditions, concurrent access
- Error handling — all errors checked, appropriate error wrapping, no silent failures
- Resource management — proper cleanup, no leaks, correct resource release
- Concurrency issues — race conditions, deadlocks, thread leaks
- Data integrity — validation, sanitization, consistent state management

Security Analysis:
- Input validation — all user inputs validated and sanitized
- Authentication/authorization — proper checks in place
- Injection vulnerabilities — SQL, command, path traversal
- Secret exposure — no hardcoded credentials or keys
- Information disclosure — error messages, logs, debug info

Simplicity Assessment:
- Direct solutions first — if simple approach works, do not use complex pattern
- No enterprise patterns for simple problems
- Question every abstraction — each interface/abstraction must solve real problem
- No scope creep — changes solve only the stated problem
- No premature optimization

Focus on defects that would cause runtime failures, security vulnerabilities, or maintainability problems.
Report problems only — no positive observations.

broad.name: implementation
broad.scope: goal coverage, wiring, completeness, logic flow
broad.prompt:
Review whether the implementation achieves the stated goal/requirement.

Core Review Responsibilities:
- Requirement coverage — does implementation address all aspects of the stated requirement? Are there edge cases or scenarios not handled?
- Correctness of approach — is the chosen approach actually solving the right problem? Could it fail to achieve the goal in certain conditions?
- Wiring and integration — is everything connected properly? Are new components registered, routes added, handlers wired, configs updated?
- Completeness — are there missing pieces that would prevent the feature from working? Missing imports, unimplemented interfaces, incomplete migrations?
- Logic flow — does data flow correctly from input to output? Are transformations correct? Is state managed properly?
- Edge cases — are boundary conditions handled? Empty inputs, null values, concurrent access, error paths?

Focus on correctness of approach, not code style.
Report problems only — no positive observations.

broad.name: simplification
broad.prompt:
Detect over-engineered and overcomplicated code — code that works but is more complex than necessary.

- Excessive abstraction layers — wrapper adds nothing, factory for single implementation, layer cake anti-pattern, DTO/mapper overkill
- Premature generalization — generic solution for specific problem, config objects for 2-3 options, plugin architecture for fixed functionality
- Unnecessary indirection — pass-through wrappers, excessive method chaining, interface wrapping primitives
- Future-proofing excess — unused extension points, versioned internal APIs, feature flags for permanent decisions
- Unnecessary fallbacks — fallback that never triggers, legacy mode kept just in case, dual implementations, silent fallbacks hiding problems
- Premature optimization — caching rarely-accessed data, custom data structures when arrays/maps work, worker pools for occasional tasks

For each finding report: location, which over-engineering pattern detected, why it adds unnecessary complexity, what simpler code would look like.
Report problems only — no positive observations.

broad.name: testing
broad.prompt:
Review test coverage and quality.

Test Existence and Coverage:
- Missing tests — new code paths without corresponding tests
- Untested error paths — error conditions not verified
- Coverage gaps — functions or branches without test coverage

Test Quality:
- Tests verify behavior, not implementation details
- Each test is independent, can run in any order
- Both success and error paths tested
- Edge cases and boundary conditions covered

Fake Test Detection:
- Tests that always pass regardless of code changes
- Tests checking hardcoded values instead of actual output
- Tests verifying mock behavior instead of code using the mock
- Ignored errors with _ or empty error checks
- Conditional assertions that always pass

Test Independence:
- No shared mutable state between tests
- Proper setup and teardown
- No order dependencies between tests
- Resources properly cleaned up

Edge Case Coverage:
- Empty inputs and collections
- Null/nil values
- Boundary values (zero, max, min)
- Concurrent access scenarios

Report problems only — no positive observations.

broad.name: documentation
broad.prompt:
Review code changes and identify missing documentation updates.

README.md (Human Documentation) — must document:
- New features or capabilities
- New CLI flags or command-line options
- New API endpoints or interfaces
- New configuration options
- Changed behavior that affects users
- New dependencies or system requirements
- Breaking changes
Skip: internal refactoring with no user-visible changes, bug fixes that restore documented behavior, test additions, code style changes.

Internal docs — must document:
- New architectural patterns discovered/established
- New conventions or coding standards
- New build/test commands
- New libraries or tools integrated
- Project structure changes
- Workflow changes
Skip: standard code additions following existing patterns, simple bug fixes, test additions using existing patterns.

Plan Files:
- Mark completed items as done
- Update plan status if needed
- Note which plan items this change addresses

Report problems only — no positive observations.

focused.name: critical-correctness
focused.scope: critical and major correctness, security, reliability
focused.prompt:
Review code only for critical and major bugs, security issues, and correctness problems.
Focus only on critical and major issues. Ignore style/minor issues.

- Logic errors that cause incorrect behavior
- Security vulnerabilities — injection, XSS, secrets exposure, improper validation
- Data loss or corruption risks
- Race conditions — concurrent access, shared state, missing synchronization
- Resource leaks — unclosed handles, missing cleanup
- Error handling gaps — silent failures, ignored errors

Report problems only — no positive observations.

focused.name: critical-coverage
focused.scope: critical and major goal coverage, integration, completeness
focused.prompt:
Review whether any critical or major requirement-coverage or integration issues remain.
Focus only on critical and major issues. Ignore style/minor issues.

- Requirements that are not implemented at all
- Integration bugs between components — missing wiring, unregistered handlers, broken data flow
- Critical logic flow errors
- Missing error paths that could cause crashes or data loss

Report problems only — no positive observations.
